name: FLUX.2
tagline: The FLUX lineage, now partly Apache
description: Black Forest Labs' successor to FLUX.1, covering text-to-image plus single- and multi-reference editing in one architecture. The distilled klein 4B runs on ~8GB VRAM under Apache-2.0, while the 32B dev model sets the open-weights quality bar under a non-commercial license.
why: The most prominent Western open-weights image family — and klein 4B is the first genuinely Apache-licensed FLUX-quality model that runs on mainstream consumer GPUs. The catch: the family mixes licenses per variant.
audience: Prosumers, researchers, and (for klein 4B) commercial users

setup.requirements: klein 4B: ~8GB VRAM. dev 32B: H100-class, or a 4090 with FP8 quantization

license.weights: Mixed: klein 4B + autoencoder Apache-2.0; klein 9B and dev 32B under the FLUX Non-Commercial License
license.notes: Verify the exact variant before shipping a product — the licenses differ checkpoint by checkpoint.

limitations: The flagship dev weights are non-commercial; distilled variants trade peak quality for speed.
take: A family portrait of the open-weights business model: give away the small one, license the big one. Read the label on each box.
